headline: Meta surges 10% on AI cloud compute push
detail: Meta is moving to sell excess AI compute capacity to enterprise customers, directly threatening pure-play AI cloud names. For your book, this is a double-edged sword: it validates AI infrastructure demand (good for SMH, AMAT, KLAC thesis) but pressures cloud-dependent names. Chip demand from hyperscalers like Meta building out massive GPU clusters is the core bull case for your semiconductor exposure.
rec: SMH is your direct proxy here - if it reclaims momentum off today's AI enthusiasm, that's the setup to watch. Scanner shows AMAT long entry around $659 with stop $656 if you want a pure-play, but only as a small add given you're already heavy semis on margin. No action required right now.

headline: CoreWeave and Nebius tumble as Meta enters cloud
detail: Meta competing in AI cloud is an existential threat to smaller cloud players. This is a sector rotation signal - compute infrastructure winners (chipmakers) may benefit but cloud resellers lose. Your semiconductor names (SMH, SOXL) are on the right side of this trade if the thesis holds.
rec: No action needed today, but this reinforces staying in chip hardware names over cloud software. Watch SMH for a direction tell - if it can't rally on this AI news, your SOXL position at -11% becomes more urgent to manage.

headline: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI and Anthropic token model
detail: This is noise for your specific holdings but signals ongoing volatility and narrative battles in the AI space. It does not directly impact your chip or index names. Relevant only as a reminder that AI sentiment can shift fast on headlines.
rec: Nothing in your book is directly exposed to this. Skip it.

headline: Warsh tells Wall Street to stop watching Fed, watch economic data
detail: This is a macro signal that rate-cut expectations should not be driving positioning right now - focus on actual growth data. For a margin trader concentrated in high-multiple tech and chips, this matters: if economic data disappoints, your leveraged names (SOXL, SPAL) get hit hardest and fastest. It raises the bar for the macro tailwind your book needs.
rec: No trade, but keep your stops tight across all leveraged positions. A macro data surprise to the downside hits your book disproportionately given margin exposure. This is a reason to not add new leveraged positions today.
